dm_toolbox.title: DM's Toolbox
dm_toolbox.description: Practical tools and advice for adventure elements, backgrounds, and combat design

creating_backgrounds.title: Creating a Custom Background
creating_backgrounds.overview: How to create backgrounds tailored for your world and campaign themes
purpose: Help player craft the story they have in mind

step_by_step.title: Choose Abilities
step_by_step.description: Choose three abilities that seem appropriate for the background

options.use: Ideal for backgrounds involving physical exertion
Ideal for backgrounds involving endurance or long hours of activity
Ideal for backgrounds focusing on cerebral or spiritual matters
Ideal for backgrounds involving performance or social interaction

step_by_step.title: Choose a Feat
step_by_step.description: Choose one feat from the Origin category
step_by_step.note: See Player's Handbook for examples of Origin feats

step_by_step.title: Choose Skill Proficiencies
step_by_step.description: Choose two skills appropriate for the background
step_by_step.note: No need for relationship between skill proficiencies and ability score increases

step_by_step.title: Choose a Tool Proficiency
step_by_step.description: Choose one tool used in practice of background or often associated with it

combat_encounters.title: Combat Encounters
combat_encounters.overview: Features and design principles for interesting combat

combat_encounter_features.title: Interesting Combat Encounter Features
combat_encounter_features.overview: Features that make combat encounters more interesting or challenging

features.description: Enemies in hard-to-reach locations or defensive positions
features.benefit: Force ranged attackers to move around

features.description: Different types of monsters working together

combat_encounter_difficulty.title: Combat Encounter Difficulty
combat_encounter_difficulty.overview: Creating encounters of desired difficulty level

characteristics: Likely to have one or two scary moments
Characters should emerge victorious with no casualties
One or more might need to use healing resources
categories.guideline: Single monster generally presents low-difficulty challenge for party of four characters whose level equals monster's CR

characteristics: Absent healing and resources, could go badly for adventurers
Weaker characters might get taken out of fight
Slim chance one or more characters might die
Could be lethal for one or more characters
Characters need smart tactics, quick thinking, maybe luck to survive

xp_budget_system.title: XP Budget System for Encounter Design
xp_budget_system.overview: Step-by-step process for building encounters of specific difficulty

step_1.title: Choose a Difficulty
step_1.action: Select Low, Moderate, or High difficulty

step_2.title: Determine Your XP Budget
process: Cross-reference party's level with desired difficulty in table
Multiply the number by number of characters in party
Result is your XP budget for the encounter

xp_budget_table.title: XP Budget per Character by Party Level and Difficulty

step_3.title: Spend Your Budget
process: Every creature has an XP value in its stat block
When adding creature, deduct its XP from budget
Spend as much of budget as possible without going over
It's OK to have a few unspent XP left over

possible_encounters.composition: 2 Adult Red Dragons and 2 Fire Giants

encounter_troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting Combat Encounters
encounter_troubleshooting.overview: Keep these considerations in mind when creating and running encounters

issues.concern: More creatures in encounter = higher risk of lucky streak dealing more damage than expected
issues.guideline: If encounter includes more than two creatures per character, include fragile creatures that can be defeated quickly

scenario: Die rolls result in easier/harder encounter than intended

issues.description: Creatures with CR 0, particularly ones worth 0 XP
issues.tip: If wanting to include many CR 0 critters, use swarms instead

issues.description: Best encounters pair one kind of creature with another
issues.concern: Referencing more than two or three stat blocks can be daunting

issues.description: Combat encounter includes creature with CR higher than party's level
issues.concern: Such creature might deal enough damage with single action to take out one or more characters
issues.example: Ogre (CR 2) can kill level 1 Wizard with single blow

issues.description: Monster has feature that lower-level characters can't easily overcome
issues.guidance: Consider not adding that monster to encounter for characters whose level is lower than monster's CR
